content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 17,
1994
Elisa Diaz
1804 East Mesa Verde Avenue
San Bernardino,
CA
92404
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—94-020
Dear Ms.
Diaz:
I
am writing to confirm our telephone conversation on
February 7,
1994,
wherein we discussed your request for written
advice concerning the proper use of your campaign funds and the
“personal use” provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
QUESTIONS
You are a board member of the San Bernardino City Unified
School District Board (“district”).
1.
You would like to hold parent/constituent meetings to
discuss issues regarding the school district.
May you use your
campaign funds for invitations,
postage and refreshments for such
meetings?
2.
May you use campaign funds to donate to charitable or
educational organizations?
3.
May you be reimbursed from campaign funds for vehicle
mileage expenses incurred in the performance of duties concerning
your office?
4.
May you use campaign funds to pay a portion of your
monthly residential telephone bill for long distance calls made in
connection with your office,
ie.
to the State Department of
Education and the FPPC?
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


File No.
A—94--020
Page
2
5.
You would like to attend a conference on bilingual
education,
which is being held in Phoenix, Arizona.
May you use
campaign funds for registration, airfare,
food and lodging?
CONCLUS IONS
1.
Campaign.funds may be used to send invitations and host
parent/constituent meetings to discuss school district issues.
Such expenditures are associated with holding office
(Section
89512)
2.
Section 89515 allows the use of campaign funds for
donations as follows:
Campaign funds may be used to make donations or
loans to bona fide charitable,
educational, civic,
religious,
or similar tax—exempt,
nonprofit
organizations,
where no substantial part of the proceeds
will have a material financial effect on the candidate,
elected officer,
campaign treasurer,
or any individual
or individuals with authority to approve the expenditure
of campaign funds held by a committee,
or member of his
or her immediate family,
and where the donation or loan
bears a reasonable relation to a political,
legislative,
or governmental purpose.
3.
Section 89516(d)
allows for the use of campaign funds for
vehicle expense as follows:
(d)
Campaign funds may be used to reimburse a
candidate,
elected officer, his or her immediate family,
or any individual or individuals with authority to
approve the expenditure of campaign funds held by a
committee,
or an employee or member of the staff of the
committee or of the elected officer’s governmental
agency,
for the use of his or her vehicle at the rate
approved by the Internal Revenue Service pursuant to
Section 162 of the Internal Revenue Code in connection
with deductible mileage expenses under the federal
income tax law, if both of the following requirements
are met:
(1)
The vehicle use for which reimbursement is
sought is directly related to political, governmental,
or legislative purposes.
(2)
The specific purpose and mileage in connection
with each expenditure is documented in a manner approved
by the Internal Revenue Service in connection with
deductible mileage expenses.


File No.
A—94—020
Page
3
4.
Section 89511.5
(copy enclosed)
provides that an
incumbent elected officer may use personal funds for certain
expenditures and may be reimbursed from campaign funds under
certain conditions.
If these conditions are met, it appears that
you may be reimbursed for the long distance telephone calls that
are made in connection with your office.
Please refer to the
enclosed statute or specific guidelines.
5.
As a school district board member,
attendance at the
conference on bilingual education would be considered directly
related to a governmental purpose.
Section 89513(a)
allows
expenditures for travel as follows:
(a)
Campaign funds shall not be used to pay or
reimburse the candidate,
the elected officer,
or any
individual or individuals with authority to approve the
expenditure of campaign funds held by a committee,
or
employees or staff of the committee or the elected
officer’s governmental agency for travel expenses and
necessary accommodations except when these expenditures
are directly related to a political,
legislative,
or
governmental purpose.
(1)
For the purposes of this section,
payments or
reimbursements for travel and necessary accommodations
shall be considered as directly related to a political,
legislative,
or governmental purpose if the payments
would meet standards similar to the standards of. the
Internal Revenue Service pursuant to Sections
162
and
274 of the Internal Revenue Code for deductions of
travel expenses under the federal
income tax law.
(2)
For the purposes of this section,
payments or
reimbursement for travel by the household of
a candidate
or elected officer when traveling to the same
destination in order to accompany the candidate or
elected officer shall be considered for the same purpose
as the candidate’s or elected officer’s travel.
(3)
Whenever campaign funds are used to pay or
reimburse a candidate,
elected officer, his or her
representative,
or a member of the candidate’s household
for travel expenses and necessary accommodations,
the
expenditure shall be reported as required by paragraph
(7)
of subdivision (j)
of Section 84211.
(4)
Whenever campaign funds are used to pay or
reimburse for travel expenses and necessary
accommodations,
any mileage credit which is earned or
awarded pursuant to an airline bonus mileage program
shall be deemed personally earned by or awarded to the
individual traveler.
Neither the earning or awarding of
mileage credit,
nor the redeeming of credit for actual


File No.
A—94—020
Page
4
travel,
shall be subject to reporting pursuant to
Section 84211.
As a school board member,
attendance at the conference on
bilingual education would be considered directly related to a
governmental purpose.
Therefore,
campaign funds may be used to
pay for registration, airfare,
food and lodging at the conference.
In addition,
the tatute allows for the payment of travel expenses
and necessary accommodations for your husband if he accompanies
you to the conference.
(Section 89513(a)(2).)
However,
no part
of expenditures for solely personal purposes may be paid for with
campaign funds.
(Bass Advice Letter,
No.
A—90-474,
copy
enclosed.)
I trust this answers your questions.
If you have any further
questions,
you may contact me at
(916)
322—5901.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
I
7’
By: Jill\’
-
Counel,
Legal Division
SGC : JS: ak
Enclosures
